data_in.description: Python 3 interpreter with pip package manager available

data_out.description: Installation validation report (text or JSON) confirming successful pip installation and listing import status (success/failure) for each of the five core modules

domain_knowledge: Pyteomics is a Python collection providing modules for proteomics data analysis including physico-chemical property calculation and access to proteomics data formats.

The five core modules (pyteomics.mass, pyteomics.pepxml, pyteomics.mzid, pyteomics.tandem, pyteomics.auxiliary) represent distinct functional areas within the package for handling different proteomics data types and calculations.
pip is the standard Python package manager used to install packages from PyPI, the public Python package repository.
Successful module import in Python requires not only package installation but also correct environment configuration and absence of unmet dependency conflicts.

direct_checks: verify file pyteomics exists after pip installation (package directory or site-packages location)
script_runs: execute 'python -c "import pyteomics.mass; import pyteomics.pepxml; import pyteomics.mzid; import pyteomics.tandem; import pyteomics.auxiliary"' and confirm exit code is 0
verify no ImportError or ModuleNotFoundError is raised during core module imports
verify each of the five core modules (pyteomics.mass, pyteomics.pepxml, pyteomics.mzid, pyteomics.tandem, pyteomics.auxiliary) is accessible as a Python importable object after installation
expert_review: confirm that installed version of Pyteomics is compatible with the versions of declared dependencies (numpy, lxml, matplotlib, pandas, sqlalchemy, pynumpress) present in the environment

cmd: pip install Pyteomics && python -c "import sys; modules = ['pyteomics.mass', 'pyteomics.pepxml', 'pyteomics.mzid', 'pyteomics.tandem', 'pyteomics.auxiliary']; results = {}; [results.update({m: 'success'}) if __import__(m) or True else results.update({m: 'failed'}) for m in modules]; import json; print(json.dumps({'installation': 'success', 'module_imports': results}, indent=2))"

Produce a validation report confirming installation success and module availability.

workflow_description: 1. Install Pyteomics using pip by executing `pip install pyteomics` in a Python 3 environment. 2. Verify installation by attempting sequential imports of pyteomics.mass, pyteomics.pepxml, pyteomics.mzid, pyteomics.tandem, and pyteomics.auxiliary modules. 3. Log import success/failure status for each module and generate a validation report documenting installation completion and module readiness.
